text.title: Code Review Process Rules
text.summary: PR size limits, CI gate order, review checklist categories, and CODEOWNERS routing.

rule.statement: PRs must contain <400 changed lines and <20 files. Larger PRs must be split.
rule.rationale: Industry data (SmartBear, Cisco) shows defect detection drops sharply above 400 lines and 60-90 minutes of review time. Reviewers rubber-stamp large PRs regardless of stated intent.

rule.statement: CI must be green (lint + tests + types + coverage) before a PR is opened for review. Reviewers do not debug build failures.
rule.rationale: Reviewers focusing on a broken build miss logic issues. Green CI is the contract that allows the reviewer to focus on design and correctness.

rule.statement: Review for: correctness, design, tests, security, naming, observability. Delegate style to the formatter (Prettier, ruff format, gofmt).
rule.rationale: Style debates in code review waste both reviewer and author time. Automated formatters decide style consistently without discussion. This frees reviewers for higher-value concerns.

list.item: Correctness — logic errors, edge cases, off-by-ones, null dereferences
Design — single responsibility, coupling, API backward compatibility
Tests — coverage of the new behavior, not just existence of tests
Security — auth checks, injection, IDOR, data exposure
Naming — clarity and consistency with project conventions

section.title: Reviewer Routing

rule.statement: Use CODEOWNERS to auto-assign reviewers by path. Do not rely on manual reviewer selection.
rule.rationale: Manual reviewer selection creates bottlenecks on popular engineers and means PRs in unfamiliar paths get no expert review. CODEOWNERS matches expertise to code automatically.

section.reference: Assigns least-loaded reviewer from CODEOWNERS via gh CLI

rule.statement: Every review comment is a blocker until the author resolves it and the reviewer confirms. No "ack" merges with open conversations.
rule.rationale: Unresolved conversations indicate disagreement or uncertainty. Merging without resolution treats every comment as optional, undermining the review process.
